hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will improve steric complementarity in the SSTR2-specific TM5/TM6 pocket, thereby increasing the selectivity margin over off-target SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be improved by disrupting conserved-pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. The next iteration will focus on these positions with guided mutations to increase Δmargin and reduce off-target binding.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to form conserved-pocket contacts with SSTR3 and SSTR5. Focusing design on these positions allows us to disrupt off-target interactions while preserving pharmacophore positions (7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
Introducing mutations at these positions with residues that are less likely to form conserved-pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 will reduce off-target binding and increase Δmargin.
